PDS Element: Core Functionality: Effective Particle and Debris Removal
Justification: The primary function is to effectively remove particulate matter and debris from various surfaces, measured under standardized conditions.; Suction power: Target of at least 200 Air Watts (AW) measured with the standard hose, wand, and a clean filter, on the highest power setting.; Airflow rate: Target of at least 35 Liters per second (L/s) measured at 20 hPa (hectopascals) with the standard hose, wand, and a clean filter.; Filtration efficiency: Must achieve HEPA H13 standard, capturing 99.97% of particles down to 0.3 microns, with specific attention to efficiency for particles <1 micron.; Dirt pickup performance: Target of 95% pickup of fine dust (ISO 11654 standard test dust) on sealed hard floors and 90% pickup of embedded dirt (e.g., standardized sand) on low-pile carpets (ISO 16092 standard test).; Pet hair removal: Target of at least 90% removal efficiency of mixed short and long pet hair from loop-pile carpets and flat-weave upholstery, tested according to internal methodology.; Debris handling: Ability to pick up at least 90% of larger debris (e.g., oats, rice, small pebbles up to 1 cm diameter) from hard floors and low-pile carpets without clogging the main airflow path.

Minimize environmental impact through reduced energy consumption during operation, based on standardized usage.; Energy consumption: Target of under 30 kWh/year, calculated based on IEC 60312-1 standard usage patterns (e.g., 1 hour/week of operation).; Operational power consumption: Standard mode not to exceed 700 Watts, achieving at least 150 AW suction; Eco mode not to exceed 400 Watts, achieving at least 80 AW suction.; Motor efficiency: Utilize a high-efficiency brushless DC motor with a minimum efficiency rating of 85% at the primary operating load point.; Aerodynamic optimization: Employ Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) analysis to optimize internal ducting and minimize turbulence, aiming for a ducting efficiency of >90%.

Prioritize materials with minimal environmental impact, enabling a circular economy through recyclability and responsible sourcing.; Recycled content: Minimum of 50% post-consumer recycled (PCR) plastic content across all major plastic components (e.g., PP, ABS). Specific targets: PP ≥ 60% PCR, ABS ≥ 40% PCR.; Recyclability: All plastic components must be clearly marked with recycling codes and be readily recyclable through standard municipal streams. Design for disassembly to facilitate material sorting.; Hazardous substances: Strict adherence to RoHS and REACH regulations, avoiding heavy metals and other restricted substances.; Design for disassembly: Components joined using common fasteners (e.g., Phillips head screws) or easily disengageable snap-fits. Avoid permanent adhesives where possible.; Material sourcing: Prioritize suppliers with transparent supply chains, requiring certification for recycled content and ethical labor practices.; Bio-based materials: Limited use of certified bio-based plastics for non-critical components where durability and UV resistance are not compromised. Avoid biodegradable plastics unless industrial composting is guaranteed.

Extend product lifespan to reduce waste and the need for frequent replacements, supported by rigorous testing.; Expected product lifespan: Designed for a minimum of 7 years of typical household usage, validated through accelerated life testing simulating 3,500 operational hours.; Component durability: Brush roll motor rated for a minimum of 5,000 operational hours; main motor rated for 3,500 operational hours under specified load conditions.; Drop test: Withstand a drop from 1 meter onto a 2cm thick concrete surface without loss of functionality or significant structural damage.; Cycle testing: Brush roll tested for 100,000 cycles (simulating entanglement and disentanglement); dustbin emptying mechanism tested for 20,000 cycles.; Spare parts availability: Critical spare parts available for a minimum of 10 years after product discontinuation, with clear online instructions for replacement.

PDS Element: User Experience: Ease of Use and Ergonomics
Justification: Ensure comfortable, intuitive, and efficient operation for a wide range of users.; Main unit weight: Maximum 3 kg for the primary handheld/body unit, with balanced weight distribution assessed through user handling tests.; Maneuverability: Swivel steering with a minimum turning radius of 180 degrees, requiring less than 5 N of force to steer on low-pile carpet.; Cord length: Minimum 8-meter cord with a reliable, automatic retraction system activated by a single button press.; Dustbin emptying: Single-hand operation for dustbin removal and emptying, completed in under 10 seconds with dust emission measured below 5 mg/m³ using a standardized test chamber.; Control interface: Intuitive controls with a maximum of three primary function buttons (Power On/Off, Power Mode Select) featuring clear tactile feedback.; Clearance height: Ability to clean under furniture with a minimum clearance height of 10 cm.; User testing: Design validated through user testing with diverse demographics (age, gender, physical ability) using defined task completion metrics.

Minimize operational noise to enhance user comfort and reduce disturbance.; Maximum noise level: Not to exceed 70 dB(A) sound pressure level (SPL) on the standard power setting, measured at 1.5 meters from the unit on a hard floor.; Eco mode noise level: Not to exceed 65 dB(A) SPL on the eco power setting.; Psychoacoustic comfort: Minimize high-frequency (above 4 kHz) and low-frequency (below 100 Hz) noise components to reduce perceived annoyance.

Simplify and expedite routine maintenance tasks for optimal performance and longevity.; Tool-free access: All user-serviceable parts (filters, brush roll) accessible without tools.; Filter maintenance: Target time for filter replacement under 2 minutes.; Brush roll cleaning: Easy access for clearing tangled hair and debris, or a self-cleaning mechanism.; Filter lifespan: HEPA filter rated for a minimum of 12 months of normal usage (e.g., weekly cleaning of pre-filters); pre-motor filters rated for 6 months.; Maintenance guidance: Clear, visually supported instructions provided in the user manual and via an optional AR overlay through a mobile app.

Ensure superior indoor air quality by effectively capturing fine particles and allergens throughout the filter's lifespan.; HEPA standard: Minimum HEPA H13 filtration efficiency (99.97% at 0.3 microns).; Pressure drop: Filtration system designed for a maximum pressure drop of 25 hPa at 35 L/s airflow to maintain suction.; Performance maintenance: Filtration system designed to maintain performance over its lifespan, with clear indicators for replacement.

Provide adequate capacity for typical cleaning sessions and ensure hygienic, efficient emptying.; Minimum dustbin capacity: 1.5 liters for bagless models, sufficient for average household cleaning needs.; Hygienic emptying: Dust re-emission during emptying measured below 5 mg/m³ in a controlled environment.; Single-hand operation: Dustbin removal and reattachment achievable with a single hand.; Bagged option (if applicable): Bags made of multi-layer, high-filtration material (minimum H12 equivalent) with a secure sealing mechanism for disposal.

Guarantee user safety during operation and handling through compliance and protective mechanisms.; Safety certifications: Compliance with IEC 60335-1 and relevant regional standards (e.g., CE, UL, FCC).; Overheat protection: Automatic motor thermal cut-off at 105°C, with a passive reset function after cooling.; Electrical safety: Insulation resistance > 20 MΩ; leakage current < 0.75 mA.; Cord safety: Durable cord material (minimum 1000 flex cycles), strain relief at connection points, and securely fitted plug.; Stability: Design to prevent tipping during normal use and when the hose/wand is stored.

Ensure the total cost of owning and operating the vacuum cleaner is competitive and justifiable.; Target purchase price: MSRP between $350 - $450 USD.; Estimated annual operating cost: Not to exceed $25 USD (based on electricity cost of $0.15/kWh, 1 hour/week usage, and annual filter replacement).; Consumable cost: Replacement HEPA filter MSRP ≤ $30 USD; replacement brush roll MSRP ≤ $25 USD, with availability guaranteed for 10 years.

Create a visually appealing product that communicates its sustainable and high-quality attributes.; Design style: Minimalist, modern aesthetic with clean lines, smooth transitions, and a focus on functional form.; Material finish: Predominantly matte finishes for plastic components, with brushed aluminum or sustainable wood accents for premium feel.; Color palette: Primary colors: muted earth tones (e.g., slate grey, forest green). Accent colors: natural metallic or wood tones.; Tactile feedback: Controls and interaction points will have satisfying, deliberate tactile feedback (e.g., a distinct click for buttons).

Facilitate responsible disposal and maximize material recovery at the end of the product's life.; Disassembly time: Designed for disassembly for recycling, achievable with a standard Phillips head screwdriver in under 15 minutes.; Material labeling: All plastic and metal components >20 cm² clearly labeled with material type using industry-standard symbols (e.g., ISO 11469).; Take-back program: Investigate feasibility of a manufacturer take-back program for responsible recycling, focusing on logistics and partnerships.

Minimize the environmental footprint of product packaging through material choice and design.; Recycled content: Minimum 80% post-consumer recycled content in all cardboard and paper-based packaging.; Plastic reduction: Eliminate single-use plastics; utilize molded pulp or honeycomb cardboard for protective elements.; FSC Certification: All paper and cardboard products must be FSC certified.; Packaging volume: Packaging volume not to exceed 40% of the product's volume.; Packaging weight: Packaging weight not to exceed 15% of the product's net weight.; Transit protection: Packaging must meet ISTA 3A or equivalent transit testing requirements.

Provide a comprehensive cleaning solution for diverse household needs and surfaces.; Included attachments: Crevice tool, upholstery brush, and a combination floor tool with adjustable settings for carpets and hard floors.; Motorized brush head: Include a powered brush head with adjustable RPM (e.g., 2000-4000 RPM) for deep carpet cleaning and pet hair removal.; Attachment storage: Onboard storage for crevice tool and upholstery brush; optional wall-mountable storage rack for the main unit and other accessories.; Surface adaptation: Floor heads feature an adjustable height mechanism or automatic sensor to adapt to different floor types.; Variable suction power: Minimum of 4 distinct power levels, clearly indicated, allowing users to select optimal settings.
